items.title: Ramanan congratulates Vijay, likens Tamil Nadu victory to Anwar's political journey
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has congratulated C. Joseph Vijay on his appointment as Tamil Nadu chief minister, likening his rise to the political journey of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Human Resources minister said the film star-turned-politician's victory symbolised the resurgence of people's struggle and showed perseverance and courage in upholding principles would ultimately earn a strong mandate from society. "Vijay's political journey reminds me of the long struggle of Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who endured various challenges, pressures and political persecution for more than two decades before eventually being appointed as Malaysia's 10th prime minister.

items.title: WHO chief warns hantavirus fight 'not over' after cruise ship evacuation
items.summary: MADRID: World Health Organization chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Tuesday "our work is not over" to contain hantavirus after evacuations from a cruise ship hit by a deadly outbreak of the illness. The fate of the MV Hondius has sparked international alarm after three passengers died in an outbreak of the rare virus, for which no vaccines or specific treatments exist. More than 120 passengers and crew on the MV Hondius were flown out from Spain's Canary Islands on Sunday and Monday, and countries have adopted different health measures for their returning evacuees.

items.title: On final day as MACC chief, Azam Baki rules out politics and business in next chapter
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has officially ruled out a career in politics or the private sector, vowing instead to dedicate his retirement to lifelong public service. Speaking to reporters after his retirement ceremony today, Azam addressed long-standing speculation regarding his post-retirement plans, insisting he has no intention of transitioning into a businessman or a politician, despite being frequently asked about such prospects. Azam officially ended his duties today, marking the conclusion of a high-profile chapter in Malaysian law enforcement.

items.title: Anwar orders all ministries to retarget aid and protect vulnerable Malaysians from rising costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has issued a direct order to all ministries to realign their initiatives and assistance programmes, ensuring that government resources are precision-targeted to shield the poor and vulnerable from the escalating global supply crisis. Chairing the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting today, Anwar said that as the crisis puts increasing pressure on the prices of daily goods, it is imperative that no citizen is left behind. “Taking into account the impact of the crisis, which is increasingly putting pressure on the prices of daily goods, I have instructed every ministry to realign initiatives, efforts and assistance so that they reach the target groups and ensure no one is left behind, especially the poor and vulnerable groups,” he said in a statement online.

items.title: Israel sent Iron Dome air defence systems to UAE, US envoy says
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Israel has sent batteries for its Iron Dome air defence system along with personnel to operate them to the United Arab Emirates, US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee said on Tuesday. "Israel just sent them (the UAE) Iron Dome batteries and personnel to help them operate them. "Because there's an extraordinary relationship between the UAE and Israel based on the Abraham Accords," Huckabee said at an event in Tel Aviv, referring to a 2020 deal that saw Israel establish ties with the UAE.

items.title: ‘No sabotage, please’: Azam Baki tells MACC to show absolute loyalty to successor
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has issued a stern reminder to his officers to extend their full, unwavering support to his successor, Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman. “There must be no sabotage of the new chief commissioner's leadership. Full cooperation must be given to the incoming leadership,” Azam said during his farewell address.

items.title: Azam Baki reflects on family sacrifice as he ends 42-year MACC career
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Tan Sri Azam Baki ended his 42-year enforcement career today, reflecting on decades of public service and paying tribute to his family. Fighting back tears during his farewell and clocking-out ceremony at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters here, Azam said his family had faced challenges they "should never have had to face". Azam said he remained grateful that his family stayed patient and composed through the difficult moments of his career.

items.title: ‘Taken out of context’: Liew Chin Tong denies announcing new 150L Budi95 limit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong has clarified that he did not announce a reduced 150-litre Budi95 quota, saying that his remarks during a corporate event earlier today were taken out of context. The clarification follows media reports suggesting that the government was mulling specific fuel caps as part of its subsidy rationalisation strategy. Addressing the "media storm" caused by the reports, Liew explained that his comments were focused on data and long-term sustainability.

items.title: Thunderstorms forecast for KL, Selangor, and majority of states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Commuters and residents across the Klang Valley and several other states are advised to brace for heavy downpours and strong winds as the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued a widespread thunderstorm warning valid until 7:00 pm today. In the central region, the warning covers Kuala Lumpur and several districts in Selangor, specifically Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat. The weather warning extends significantly across the east coast and southern peninsula.

items.title: Founding was only half the story: Descendant says Yap Ah Loy’s true legacy was rebuilding KL after war
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Yap Ah Loy is widely remembered as the man who founded modern Kuala Lumpur, but according to his fifth-generation great-grandson Glenn Yap, that story only tells half the truth. Glenn said Yap’s defining contribution was not only founding the city, but rebuilding it after Kuala Lumpur was reduced to ruins during the Klang War, which ended in 1873, and again after a major fire and severe flooding struck the town in 1881. “People always say Yap Ah Loy founded Kuala Lumpur, but what made him extraordinary was that he rebuilt KL after it was completely destroyed during the Klang War,” he said during the Life and Legacy of Yap Ah Loy talk organised by Badan Warisan Malaysia.

items.title: 'Don't sabotage the new chief,' Azam Baki tells MACC staff in farewell address
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: On his final day as chief commissioner, Tan Sri Azam Baki today had a message for Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) officers and staff: "Don't sabotage the new chief commissioner." In his farewell address after six years at the helm, Azam called on the commission to give their full support to the new leadership and senior management to ensure continuity in the agency's administration and legacy. "Please give your support to the MACC top management because they will continue our legacy."

items.title: Vijay thanks Anwar for congratulatory message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Film star-turned-politician C. Joseph Vijay has expressed appreciation to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim after receiving congratulatory remarks following his electoral success in Tamil Nadu. Vijay said he was deeply honoured by the warm congratulations extended by Anwar and thanked him for the gracious gesture. The response came after Anwar earlier congratulated Vijay on his election as chief minister of Tamil Nadu, in a post on social media.

items.title: ECRL passenger service to run shuttle trains along east coast states
items.summary: Pahang Public Works, Transport and Health Committee chairman Datuk Ir Razali Kassim said the electric trains will not operate directly from Gombak to Kota Baru, Kelantan. He said shuttle trains will stop in all three east coast states, namely Pahang, Terengganu and Kelantan. "The electric trains will depart from Gombak to Kuantan before returning to Gombak.

items.title: Investors want Trump and Xi to stay out of AI's way
items.summary: INVESTORS expect US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart to keep trade tensions on the backburner when they meet in Beijing and say they are focused on the booming AI sector and whether the US will relax chip export restrictions. There's little China is eager to discuss with Trump," said Yang Tingwu, vice general manager of Tongheng Investment, adding that Trump's unresolved war with Iran has weakened his hand. Yang has invested in China Mobile and China Telecom for exposure to their data-centre businesses.

items.title: Umno opens doors to ousted Bersatu leaders via Rumah Bangsa initiative, says party veep
items.summary: KLUANG, May 12 — Umno is ready to welcome former Bersatu members and leaders subjected to disciplinary action by their party to join its ranks through the Rumah Bangsa initiative, said party Vice-President Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. The Rumah Bangsa Unification Committee chairman said that as a party that serves as a home for the Malays, Umno remains open to anyone who wishes to align with the party’s struggle. Yesterday, the Bersatu Disciplinary Board sacked and suspended four more elected representatives and party leaders for violating Clause 9.1.4 of the party constitution, as well as the party’s Code of Ethics and Conduct.

items.title: Starmer defies calls to quit, says he is getting on with governing
items.summary: LONDON: Prime Minister Keir Starmer defied calls to resign on Tuesday, telling ministers he would "get on with governing" despite a "destabilising" 48 hours of growing calls to set out a timetable for his departure after an election drubbing. At a meeting of his cabinet team of ministers, Starmer, in the top job for less than two years, repeated that while he took responsibility for one of his Labour Party's worst election defeats, there had been no official move to trigger a leadership contest. "The country expects us to get on with governing.

items.title: Nineteen illegal factories in Pulau Indah demolished after operating for over 20 years
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 12 — A total of 19 illegal factories in Pulau Indah, Klang have been demolished including utility disconnection through the Integrated Demolition Enforcement Operation to ensure industry compliance with Selangor state land laws. Selangor Local Government and Tourism Exco Datuk Ng Sue Lim said the operation, which involved 300 personnel from various agencies, was carried out under Section 425 of the National Land Code against premises found to be operating without proper compliance. Several land expropriation applications received around 2019 to 2020 were also rejected,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: MACC: Senior executive remanded seven days over alleged RM8,000 graft in Pahang
items.summary: “Initial investigations found that the suspect is believed to have committed the offence in 2024 by assisting a construction company and a contractor company in obtaining contracts, approving work contract evaluations, securing work completion certifications, and expediting payment claims. A seven-day remand order, until May 18, against the suspect was issued by Magistrate Tengku Eliana Tuan Kamaruzaman after an application by the MACC at the Kuantan Magistrate’s Court this morning. Meanwhile, Pahang MACC director Mohd Shukor Mahmud, when contacted, confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 16(a)(A) of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Iran warns US to accept peace terms or face 'failure'
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's chief negotiator on Tuesday issued an ultimatum to the United States to accept the conditions in Teheran's 14-point proposal for peace in the Middle East war or face "failure." The defiant message came after US President Donald Trump rejected the latest counteroffer from Iran and said that a fragile ceasefire in place since April 8 was on "life support." Iran's foreign ministry said its response called for ending the war on all fronts, including Lebanon, halting the US naval blockade on Iranian ports and securing the release of Iranian assets frozen abroad under longstanding sanctions.

items.title: Energy market shows signs of stability: Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The global energy market is showing signs of stabilisation, although the situation must continue to be closely monitored due to the risk of new developments affecting energy and production costs. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said between May 5 and May 8, the average Brent crude price fell 8.5 per cent to US$108.40 per barrel compared with US$118.50 per barrel in the previous week. However, Akmal said the level remains significantly higher than the US$68.84 per barrel recorded before the global energy crisis erupted on Feb 26, 2026.

items.title: Want to watch World Cup 2026 matches for free? Download ‘RTM Klik’ app, Fahmi tells Malaysians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — The public is encouraged to download the “RTM Klik” application to ensure they can watch the Fifa World Cup 2026 matches for free. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the tournament would be aired by Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) and Unifi TV as the official broadcasters of the Fifa World Cup 2026 in Malaysia. Last week, Fahmi said the Fifa World Cup 2026, to run from June 11 to July 19, would be broadcast on various platforms, including free-to-air broadcasts via MyTV as well as streaming platforms through RTM Klik and Unifi TV to ensure comprehensive access.

items.title: 'Shut it down': Ex-MAG chief Capt Izham says bankruptcy was considered during airline's darkest period
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) managing director Datuk Captain Izham Ismail revealed that bankruptcy was among several options considered as part of efforts to restructure Malaysia Airlines, alongside adopting recovery models used by other international carriers. He said he had discussed it with the airline's chief financial officer, Boo Hui Yee, given that the airline was in dire financial and operational condition when Izham took over in 2017, burdened by a RM25.7 billion debt, negative cash flow and poor aircraft performance. Izham said the organisation had also become deeply dysfunctional, with employees clinging to nostalgia over the airline's past glory instead of confronting present realities.

items.title: Penang assembly erupts over zakat fund governance, alleged abuse of power
items.summary: The opposition lawmaker questioned a RM1 million allocation to a company, stressing that zakat funds must strictly benefit the eight asnaf groups recognised under Islamic law. "Zakat has clear categories of recipients. Yusni also demanded clarification over land acquisitions involving zakat funds and questioned whether zakat money had been used to finance an official trip to Egypt in February 2025.

items.title: Police forum targets cybercrime, scams and illegal e-waste threats, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) is continuing to enhance the nation’s security preparedness through the organisation of the PDRM Special Dialogue V 2026 (PSD V 2026) in the efforts to address the increasingly complex and dynamic evolution of transborder crime threats. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the international-level dialogue platform represents a progressive step by the force to bring together technology experts, policymakers and enforcement agencies to formulate effective strategies against contemporary security threats. The programme also involved several key PDRM departments, including the Criminal Investigation Department, Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department, Commercial Crime Investigation Department, as well as the Internal Security and Public Order Department.

items.title: Defence Ministry lifts MAF procurement fund freeze after anti-leakage measures
items.summary: KLUANG, May 12 — The Defence Ministry (MINDEF) has confirmed that the freeze on procurement funds for Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) assets has been lifted following restructuring measures implemented to ensure there are no issues involving leakages or misuse of funds. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said all military asset procurement matters were now proceeding as planned. Previously, he said Mindef was discussing the status of the frozen MAF procurement funds with the Finance Ministry to ensure critical operations and preparedness were not affected.

items.title: Prices manageable but pressure building, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Food prices remain broadly under control, but rising costs may lead to pressure on consumers, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. Akmal said food prices remain within a manageable range, although signs of increases have started to emerge. However, Akmal said several items recorded increases.

items.title: Dutch Lady gets Pekat Group's aid in 'green' energy transition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd (DLMI) has partnered with Pekat Group Bhd to accelerate its transition to renewable energy. DLMI said the company had entered into a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) with Pekat Group's subsidiary Pekat Solar Sdn Bhd to deploy a rooftop solar system at its DLMI@Enstek manufacturing facility. Under the agreement, Pekat Group will design, install, own and operate the system, while DLMI will buy the electricity generated.

items.title: EU to invite Taliban officials to Brussels for migrant return talks
items.summary: BRUSSELS: The European Commission told AFP on Monday it plans to invite Taliban officials to Brussels in the near future for talks on returning migrants to Afghanistan. The visit, coordinated with Sweden, would follow two trips by European officials to Afghanistan on the same issue. Officials are now "working on a potential follow-up meeting at technical level in Brussels with the de-facto authorities in Afghanistan", a spokesperson for the European Union executive said.

items.title: Former Malaysia Airlines CEO: Alcohol served on flights to maintain commercial competitiveness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines continues to serve alcoholic beverages on selected international flights as part of efforts to remain commercially competitive as a premium global carrier. Former Malaysia Aviation Group group managing director Datuk Captain Izham Ismail said the airline had to balance religious sensitivities with commercial realities, particularly in meeting the expectations of premium international travellers and global airline partners. "It's a very difficult decision because you have partners – Japan Airlines, Singapore Airlines, British Airways, American Airlines – and they have their top-tier customers.

items.title: National Economic Action Council urges local authorities to cut rental rates for traders
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir Akmal said the move is crucial to help petty traders, night market operators and micro-entrepreneurs cope with rising operating costs, which now account for an average of seven to 58 per cent of their total business expenses. Meanwhile, he said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is offering a 50 per cent rental cut for selected hawker stalls until December 2027. For the services sector, Akmal Nasrullah said the Services Producer Price Index rose 2.1 per cent in the first quarter of 2026, driven by higher prices in the accommodation, food and beverage, and transport subsectors.

items.title: Mindef to begin MRCA evaluation in 2030 to replace Sukhoi, F-18 fleets
items.summary: KLUANG: The Defence Ministry will begin the evaluation process for the acquisition of Multi-Role Combat Aircraft (MRCA) in the 2030s to replace the nation's ageing frontline fighter jets. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the move was necessary as the Royal Malaysian Air Force's (RMAF) current F/A-18D Hornet and Sukhoi Su-30MKM aircraft are slated for decommissioning around 2035. Regarding the replacement of the Hawk lead-in fighter trainers, Khaled said the armed forces would receive 18 FA-50 light combat aircraft in stages starting at the end of this year.

items.title: Australia prepares quarantine for six healthy hantavirus cruise ship passengers
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Australia on Tuesday confirmed that all six hantavirus cruise ship passengers are "in good health" and will be placed in quarantine upon their return home, Anadolu Ajansi reported. Health Minister Mark Butler told ABC News that five Australians and one New Zealander from the hantavirus‑affected cruise ship MV Hondius were expected to arrive in the Netherlands before continuing their journey to Australia. Upon arrival in Western Australia, the passengers will enter a purpose‑built quarantine facility for about three weeks.

items.title: Drug war enforcer urges Marcos to not hand him over to ICC
items.summary: MANILA: The top enforcer of former Philippine leader Rodrigo Duterte's deadly war on drugs urged President Ferdinand Marcos Jr on Tuesday to not hand him over to the International Criminal Court, but said he was ready to face justice at home. Fearing arrest and hunkered down in his legislative office for a second day, Senator Ronald dela Rosa said he had faithfully served his country. The ICC unsealed an arrest warrant on Monday for former police chief dela Rosa, dated November, on suspicion of crimes against humanity, the same crimes 81-year-old Duterte is accused of as he awaits trial in The Hague following his arrest last year.

items.title: Freeze on military procurement funds lifted, says Khaled [WATCH]
items.summary: KLUANG: The Defence Ministry has confirmed that the freeze on procurement funds for armed forces assets has been lifted following restructuring measures to prevent leakages or misuse. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said all military asset procurement matters were now proceeding as planned. Meanwhile, on whether the ministry was reviewing procurement strategies following the global supply crisis, Khaled said the ministry was closely monitoring the situation.

items.title: Dutch hospital quarantines 12 after hantavirus cruise passengers arrive in Netherlands
items.summary: AMSTERDAM: Two planes with 28 passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship, which was hit by a hantavirus outbreak, landed in the Netherlands on Tuesday and a Dutch hospital treating a hantavirus patient quarantined 12 staffers in a preventative measure. Other passengers of different nationalities will continue on to their home countries from the Netherlands, authorities previously said. Radboudumc admitted a Hondius passenger infected with hantavirus on May 7.

items.title: Public to access global supply crisis dashboard from May 15, says Akmal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The Global Supply Crisis Monitoring Dashboard, jointly developed by the Ministry of Economy and the Department of Statistics Malaysia, is expected to be accessible to the public from May 15, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said the dashboard is intended to serve as a transparent and easily accessible central reference point for the public to monitor current developments related to the global supply crisis. “The public-facing dashboard comprises 10 main submenus, including energy, commodities, cost of living, economic performance, foreign exchange rates and trade flows,” he said during the Global Supply Crisis Briefing today.

items.title: Ex-MAG chief suggests pricing overhaul amid fuel surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) managing director Izham Ismail has proposed a "cost-plus" pricing model for airlines amid rising fuel costs and geopolitical uncertainty. Izham said the ongoing Middle East conflict had caused jet fuel prices to surge by more than 200 per cent, placing immense pressure on carriers such as Malaysia Airlines. He said the spike in fuel costs had placed airlines in a "very difficult position", as they grappled with soaring operating expenses while consumers remained resistant to higher ticket prices.

items.title: Yakeb: Former para swimmer Koh Lee Peng has received continuous aid since 2019 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Athletes Welfare Foundation (Yakeb) clarified today that former national para swimmer Koh Lee Peng has been receiving welfare and medical assistance since 2019. Yakeb chairman Datuk Ahmad Shapawi Ismail said the foundation provided Koh with a new electric wheelchair on April 20 to facilitate her daily activities. He said Yakeb was also helping to manage Koh's treatment and coordinate appointments with a neurology specialist at Kuala Lumpur Hospital.

items.title: Woman hunting for bamboo shoots finds 5m python instead
items.summary: Baling Malaysia Civil Defence Force (CDF) officer Mohd Faizol Ab Aziz said the agency received a call from the woman's husband at noon. He said a team of three personnel was deployed to the area. He said the CDF had caught 280 snakes involving various species in Baling since January.

items.title: Trump brushes aside Taiwan concerns ahead of Xi meet
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump has said he is ready to discuss US arms sales to Taiwan during his visit to Beijing this week, as he suggested his personal chemistry with counterpart Xi Jinping would prevent a Chinese invasion of the island. Asked if the United States should keep selling weapons to Taiwan, a key irritant for Beijing, Trump did not answer directly but said on Monday: "I'm going to have that discussion with President Xi." Trump, after referencing Russia's invasion of Ukraine, said of Taiwan, "I don't think it'll happen."

items.title: Druggie remanded for filming sister in shower
items.summary: Police believe the suspect recorded the footage through a gap beneath the bathroom door without the victim's knowledge. Acting on the report, police arrested the suspect around 1.30pm yesterday. Police also seized the suspect's mobile phone, which allegedly contained the recording of the victim.

items.title: Maybank ordered to pay RM166k to fraud victim over online banking negligence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court has ordered Maybank to compensate a woman RM166,000 after it failed to stop suspicious online banking transactions that emptied money from her account. Judge Maimoonah Aid ruled that the bank was negligent after fraudsters carried out multiple unauthorised online transfers using the 47-year-old woman's online bank account in 2021. According to court documents, the money was first withdrawn from plaintiff Chan Yan Li's housing loan account and transferred into her savings account before being sent in stages to several third-party bank accounts between June 26 and July 2, 2021.

items.title: 'Wangsa Maju sinkhole likely caused by drainage system failure, not land subsidence'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The sinkhole in Jalan Wangsa Delima 4, Wangsa Maju, is believed to have been caused by damage to the drainage system rather than land subsidence. Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is still carrying out detailed investigations together with all its utility providers to identify the exact cause of the incident. he said, referring to the damaged drainage structure found at the site.

items.title: Battersea Power Station enters next growth phase with 3.2m sq ft expansion plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Battersea Power Station's next phase of development is set to unlock up to 3.2 million square feet of new residential, commercial, cultural and leisure space across the remaining 6.5 hectares (ha) of undeveloped brownfield land at the iconic London riverside precinct. To spearhead the next evolution of the development, the Battersea Power Station Development Company (BPSDC) has appointed the London-based architecture and urban design practice Studio Egret West as master planner to refresh and refine the original master plan conceived more than 15 years ago. Harriett Renny, director of commercial leasing at Battersea Power Station, said the development continues to gain traction as one of London's most dynamic mixed-use destinations, supported by strong visitor traffic and growing retailer interest.

items.title: MOH may add hantavirus tracker to MySejahtera if needed, says deputy minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) will add a hantavirus tracker to the MySejahtera application's Disease Tracker feature if the need arises, Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib said. She said the enhancement would enable users to access real-time information on the disease more easily, adding that the ministry would continue to monitor the situation. "We hope the situation remains under control and subsides.

items.title: Retired lady cop flees hit-and-run, tests positive for drugs after 50km chase from Negeri to Melaka
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — A 65-year-old former police sergeant who allegedly fled a hit-and-run crash led authorities on a dramatic 50km cross-border chase before being arrested and testing positive for amphetamines. The pursuit, which mobilised police units from both Negeri Sembilan and Melaka, eventually came to a halt when the suspect, who is retired, was intercepted along Jalan Bukit Beruang in Melaka. Acting Rembau OCPD Deputy Superintendent Azmi Ali stated that the ordeal began earlier that night in Taman Sedia Raja, Chembong, where the suspect’s car allegedly rammed into a van and a second car.

items.title: ILO urges fair hiring practices, says migrant workers must not be charged recruitment fees
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 12 — The International Labour Organisation (ILO) said migrant workers in Malaysia, particularly vulnerable groups, should not be burdened with recruitment fees and related costs when seeking employment abroad, The Star reported. In a statement, the ILO said the principle was central to fair and ethical recruitment practices, adding that its global definition of recruitment fees and related costs had become an internationally recognised benchmark since its adoption in 2018 and approval in 2019. “The definition establishes a comprehensive international standard, clarifying that recruitment fees and related costs refer to any fees or costs incurred in the recruitment process for workers to secure employment or placement, regardless of the manner, timing, or location of their imposition or collection,” it was reported.

items.title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9pc in March, says DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Malaysia’s labour market remained stable in March 2026, with the unemployment rate unchanged at 2.9 per cent, supported by continued expansion in employment and labour force participation, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). “The labour force increased by 0.1 per cent to 17.31 million persons in March 2026 from 17.30 million in February, while the labour force participation rate (LFPR) remained at 70.9 per cent,” he said in a statement today. Mohd Uzir said the number of employed persons also edged up by 0.1 per cent to 16.80 million persons compared with 16.79 million recorded in the previous month.

items.title: Woman, 76, duped out of RM327,600 by scammers posing as police officers
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 12 — A 76-year-old woman lost RM327,600 after falling victim to a phone scam syndicate posing as police officers. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim received a call from an individual posing as a police officer from Sarawak, who claimed that her identity card number had been linked to fraudulent activities and that her bank account was under investigation. Yahaya said the victim made 42 transactions totalling RM327,600 into seven different accounts between May 28, 2025, and March 25 this year, using her savings and loans from moneylending companies.

items.title: Utility works may have triggered Wangsa Maju sinkhole, says KL mayor; area closed two weeks for repairs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The sinkhole incident on Jalan Wangsa Delima 4 in Wangsa Maju yesterday is believed to have been caused by utility maintenance works rather than soil subsidence, according to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said investigations were ongoing to determine the exact cause of the incident, while the affected area would remain closed for two weeks for repair works. Initial inspections found that a “sump” wall, or water retention structure, within the area’s main 1.5-metre drainage system had likely collapsed.

items.title: Budi95 helps Malaysia manage fuel demand amid global uncertainty, says deputy finance minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) initiative is seen as a useful demand management tool that helps Malaysia understand national usage patterns, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. Liew said this during a fireside chat titled “Malaysia’s Response to Global Energy Crisis: Build Back Better”, moderated by The Edge ’s assistant editor Emir Zainul Ensuring continuous access to fuel supply, even at reduced levels, is important, as political and social stability will enable Malaysia to manage the crisis more confidently, he said. He emphasised that Malaysia is entering the crisis from a position of relative strength, noting that while many countries have seen downward revisions in growth projections after February, Malaysia’s outlook remains resilient.

items.title: ‘What is so difficult?’: Socso CEO questions seven gig platforms yet to submit details for automatic contributions
items.summary: AMPANG, May 12 — Seven of the 14 gig service platform providers have yet to submit their workers’ details to the Social Security Organisation (Socso), delaying the rollout of automatic contributions under the Gig Workers Act 2025. Those named as having completed the process were AirAsia Ride Sdn Bhd, Kiddocare, Eternal Meteor Sdn Bhd (GoGet), ShopeeFood Malaysia, Troopers Innovation Sdn Bhd, Fastgig Sdn Bhd and Delivery Hero Malaysia (Foodpanda) Sdn Bhd. The Gig Workers Act 2025 came into force on March 31, requiring platform providers to register gig workers with Socso and implement automatic 1.25 per cent contributions through platform transactions.

items.title: KL court frees independent preacher Fathi Naim on rape charge involving teenager
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Independent preacher Muhammad Fathi Na’im Mazlam was acquitted and discharged by the Sessions Court here today on a charge of raping a 14-year-old girl five years ago. Judge Tasnim Abu Bakar ruled that the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case against the 34-year-old, better known as Fathi Naim, at the close of the prosecution’s case. The judge said the testimony of the victim, the third prosecution witness (SP3), could not be regarded as credible after impeachment proceedings revealed material discrepancies between her testimony in this trial and a separate case at the Klang Sessions Court.

items.title: Kuantan International Airport in Gebeng expected to be completed in 2031, says state exco
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 12 — The construction of Kuantan International Airport in Gebeng, here is expected to be completed in 2031, said Pahang Investment, Industry, Science, Technology and Innovation Committee chairman Datuk Mohamad Nizar Najib. He said the project is currently undergoing finalisation on technical requirements, implementation structure and involvement of strategic investors. Mohamad Nizar said the proposed infrastructure development would be implemented through private financing and would not involve state and federal government funds and would be built on land owned by the Pahang Government Secretariat.

items.title: Beyond trade: Zahid says Malaysia, China to co-build future industries through TVET
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Malaysia and China will deepen cooperation to co-develop future industries, with Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) serving as Malaysia’s first line of readiness for the future economy, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said both countries must move beyond traditional trade, identifying electric mobility, battery technology, renewable energy, smart manufacturing, digital systems and talent development as key areas of collaboration. China brings technological depth, industrial scale and proven leadership in new energy industries.

items.title: Malaysia seeks answers after Norway halts naval missile delivery despite eight-year deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Malaysia has been left “shocked” after Norway halted the delivery of missiles ordered in 2018 for the Royal Malaysian Navy’s Littoral Combat Ships (LCS), Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said today. According to The Star, Khaled said the missiles, which were scheduled for delivery in March this year, were blocked after the Norwegian government refused to approve the export licence just days before shipment. “Export licensing decisions are handled by the Norwegian authorities.

items.title: Wet weather alert: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain in nine states until 3pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected in several areas across nine states until 3pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at noon, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Kedah are Langkawi, Sik and Baling, while in Penang, the warning covers the Barat Daya and Timur Laut districts. In Perak, the affected areas are Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak and Batang Padang.

items.title: Two safe after light aircraft accident involving emergency landing in Langkawi, says CAAM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) confirms an accident had occurred involving a Diamond Aircraft model DA-40 bearing registration number 9M-HMJ on a training flight, in Langkawi today. It said the aircraft conducted an emergency landing in the vicinity of Kuah. “The aircraft departed Langkawi International Airport at 9.54am for a scheduled training flight, and the last communication with Air Traffic Control Tower Langkawi was recorded at 10.29am,” it said in a statement.

items.title: Pollution in Sungai Kereh caused by multiple factors, not just pig farms, says Penang exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 12 — The longstanding pollution issue in Sungai Kereh in Kampung Selamat, Seberang Perai Utara, is caused by multiple factors such as agricultural activities, drainage systems and waste discharge from various sources. State local government and town and country planning committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said the pollution was not only caused by pig farming activities. H’ng said the Penang Department of Environment (DoE) had received seven complaints related to water pollution and foul odour in Sungai Kereh over the past two years, from 2024 to 2026.

items.title: Sarawak free tertiary education scheme benefits 4,694 students, intake expected to rise
items.summary: KUCHING, May 12 — A total of 4,694 students from four higher education institutions (IPTs) in Sarawak have been offered free education through the Sarawak Free Higher Education Scheme (FTES) so far. Sarawak Deputy Minister of Education, Innovation and Talent Development Datuk Dr Annuar Rapaee said that according to the breakdown, i-CATS University College involved 172 students, Curtin University Malaysia (543 students), Swinburne University of Technology Sarawak Campus (1,959 students) and Universiti Teknologi Sarawak (2,020 students). Meanwhile, Dr Annuar said a total of 1,433 students chose not to accept the free education offer.
